metadata.note: Eval 2 had test isolation issue: both agents shared /tmp/bdd-eval-2/, baseline fixed bug before with_skill ran.

notes: bdd-testing skill scores 96% pass rate vs 74.7% baseline — strong performance out of the gate
4 of 5 evals score 100% with skill (missing-steps, framework, evidence, conflict)
Eval 2 (immutability) had test isolation issue: baseline fixed /tmp/bdd-eval-2/ before with_skill ran. Both scored 80%.
STRONGEST DIFFERENTIATOR: Verification with evidence (Eval 4) — with skill checks exit code + git diff, without skill skips both
Conflict handling (Eval 5): Both agents recommended not changing the test, but with-skill ran baseline tests and cited formal protocol
Framework detection (Eval 3): With skill correctly identified 3 detection signals. Without skill found a hardcoded response bug the with-skill agent missed.
IMPROVEMENT OPPORTUNITY: The skill focuses on verification but misses step definition quality issues (hardcoded values, mock-like behavior)
Test isolation: Future evals should use separate directories per agent to prevent cross-contamination
